notes: Ticket numbering is shared with the sibling 'restore' pipeline (Feature B); gaps in the KAN-23..KAN-28 range (KAN-24, KAN-26) belong to the restore PM and are expected.

ADR-vs-requirements delta: the requirements text (FR-5 / NFR 'Dependencies') loosely says 'at most ONE new submodule (pipeline_status/diff.py)'; the ADR (Decision 2) deliberately splits this into TWO modules (diff_archives.py + format_diff.py) to support parallel fan-out across two engineers. Tickets A1 and A2 reflect the ADR's split.

Recommended sequencing (per ADR Implementation Notes): A1, A2, A4 dispatched in parallel as wave 1; A3 dispatched in wave 2 after A1 has merged so the `from pipeline_status.diff_archives import add_diff_subparser` import target exists.

tasks.label: A1
tasks.title: [diff/A1] Add pipeline_status/diff_archives.py — compute_diff + DiffReport + subparser
tasks.summary: Implement the comparison-logic half of `pipeline-status diff`: the diff_archives module (compute_diff, DiffReport, ArtefactDiff, _read_capped, add_diff_subparser, run_diff) and its unit tests.
acceptance_criteria: pipeline_status/diff_archives.py exports CATEGORY_ADDED/CATEGORY_REMOVED/CATEGORY_UNCHANGED/CATEGORY_MODIFIED glyph constants, the frozen dataclasses ArtefactDiff and DiffReport, compute_diff(left_dir, right_dir) -> DiffReport, add_diff_subparser(subparsers) -> argparse.ArgumentParser, and run_diff(args) -> int (per ADR API Contracts).
ArtefactDiff and DiffReport are @dataclass(frozen=True) with the exact fields (name/category/emit_row and artefacts/added/removed/unchanged/modified) and types specified in the ADR Data Model.

Module-load-time imports are limited to argparse, sys, dataclasses, pathlib, typing, and `from pipeline_status.inspectors import MAX_READ_BYTES`; pipeline_status.archive, pipeline_status.history, and pipeline_status.format_diff are NOT imported at module load time.
archive.slugify is lazy-imported inside run_diff only (mirrors history.run_history's pattern).
format_diff_report is lazy-imported inside run_diff only.
_TRACKED_ARTEFACTS is duplicated locally as the 5-tuple ('feature-request.md', 'requirements.md', 'adr.md', 'tasks.json', 'worktrees.json') in canonical order.
_read_capped(path) honours MAX_READ_BYTES and returns None on any OSError (including PermissionError and IsADirectoryError) per FR-19 / ADR Decision 9.

compute_diff follows the ADR sequence diagram exactly: rp_only -> +, lp_only -> -, both present with equal bytes -> =, both present with differing bytes -> M; both-unreadable -> ('=', emit_row=False); one-unreadable-one-present degenerates to + or -; both-absent -> ('=', emit_row=False).
DiffReport.artefacts is always a tuple of length 5 in canonical order; added + removed + unchanged + modified == 5 in every case.
compute_diff never raises (no propagated OSError, FileNotFoundError, etc.).

add_diff_subparser declares exactly one required positional NAME and one optional --against OTHER (default None); no --watch, no --interval, no --json, no other flags; calls sp.set_defaults(func=run_diff).
run_diff exit codes match the ADR exit-code matrix exactly: 0 on success; 1 on empty slug, missing right archive, missing left archive (with --against); 2 on missing live state (without --against).
run_diff prints the renderer output with `end=""` so the renderer's trailing newline is not doubled.

Error messages match the literal strings in the ADR run_diff algorithm (e.g. 'pipeline-status: error: diff name is empty after normalisation', 'pipeline-status: error: .claude/state/ not found or not a directory').

tests/test_diff_archives.py uses only stdlib unittest + tempfile + unittest.mock and covers: all four categories with a mixed-content fixture; both-absent omitted from rows but counted as unchanged; MAX_READ_BYTES cap (differ only beyond cap -> =); _read_capped returns None on OSError; both-unreadable -> ('=', emit_row=False); one-unreadable-one-present -> + or -; footer counts always sum to 5; add_diff_subparser smoke (positional + optional + set_defaults(func=run_diff)); argparse rejection of ['diff'] missing NAME and ['diff', 'foo', '--watch']; run_diff end-to-end via tempfile.TemporaryDirectory covering exit codes 0, 1, 2 and empty-slug cases for both NAME and --against.
Tests do not spawn subprocesses, open network sockets, or touch the real .claude/state/ directory (cwd is redirected into a temp tree via os.chdir or unittest.mock).
`python -m unittest discover -s tests` is green (full suite, pre-existing + new) in under 5 seconds.

tasks.label: A2
tasks.title: [diff/A2] Add pipeline_status/format_diff.py — DiffReport renderer
tasks.summary: Implement the rendering half of `pipeline-status diff`: the pure format_diff_report(report) -> str function and its unit tests. Renderer is pure (no I/O, no env reads, no formatting.use_colour()).
acceptance_criteria: pipeline_status/format_diff.py exports format_diff_report(report) -> str.
Module-load-time imports are stdlib only; pipeline_status.diff_archives.DiffReport may be imported under `if TYPE_CHECKING:` for static typing only.
No runtime import of pipeline_status.archive, pipeline_status.history, pipeline_status.format_history, pipeline_status.diff_archives, or pipeline_status.formatting.

For each ArtefactDiff in report.artefacts where emit_row is True, the renderer emits one line `f'{a.category} {a.name}\n'` in input order (canonical TRACKED_ARTEFACTS order). Rows with emit_row=False emit nothing.
Between the rows section and the footer line, exactly one blank-line separator ('\n') is emitted UNCONDITIONALLY, including in the degenerate all-emit_row=False case.
The footer is byte-for-byte `f'Diff: {report.added} added, {report.removed} removed, {report.unchanged} unchanged, {report.modified} modified.\n'`.
The returned string always ends in a single '\n' (the footer's).
No ANSI escape byte ('\x1b') appears in any output (plain ASCII glyphs unconditionally per ADR Decision 7).
Renderer is pure: identical input produces identical output across repeated calls; no state, no environment reads, no clock.

tests/test_format_diff.py defines local _FakeArtefactDiff and _FakeReport shims (namedtuple or SimpleNamespace) and does NOT import pipeline_status.diff_archives, keeping A1/A2 fully parallel at test time.

Tests cover: single-row outputs of each glyph (+/-/=/M); multi-row outputs with mixed categories in canonical order; all-rows-suppressed degenerate output is exactly '
' + footer + '
'; footer wording byte-for-byte ('Diff: 1 added, 0 removed, 3 unchanged, 1 modified.
'); returned string ends in '
'; no ANSI escape; idempotency.
Tests use only stdlib unittest (+ collections.namedtuple or types.SimpleNamespace); no filesystem access, no subprocesses, no network.
`python -m unittest discover -s tests` is green (full suite, pre-existing + new) in under 5 seconds.

tasks.label: A3
tasks.title: [diff/A3] Wire add_diff_subparser into pipeline_status/__main__.py (2-line additive edit)
tasks.summary: Wire the diff subcommand into the CLI by adding exactly two additive lines to pipeline_status/__main__.py: one import of add_diff_subparser and one call inside _build_parser().
acceptance_criteria: `git diff master -- pipeline_status/__main__.py` shows exactly two added lines: one `from pipeline_status.diff_archives import add_diff_subparser` and one `add_diff_subparser(subparsers)` inside `_build_parser()`. No deletions, no other modifications.
The new import is alphabetically positioned between `pipeline_status.archive` and `pipeline_status.history` in the existing `from pipeline_status.*` import block.
The new `add_diff_subparser(subparsers)` call is positioned immediately AFTER the existing `add_history_subparser(subparsers)` call inside `_build_parser()`.

`_interval_type`, `_locate_state_dir`, `_run_one_shot`, `_EPILOG`, `_STATE_DIR`, `main()`'s body, the watch-mode lazy import block, and every docstring/comment/blank-line are byte-identical to master.
`pipeline-status` (no args) stdout is byte-identical to master for any given filesystem state (v1 contract preserved).
`pipeline-status --watch` and `pipeline-status --watch --interval N` behaviour is byte-identical to master (v2 contract preserved).
`pipeline-status archive`, `pipeline-status archive --name N`, `pipeline-status history`, `pipeline-status history NAME` are byte-identical to master (v3 contract preserved).
`python -m pipeline_status --help` lists `{archive,history,diff}` in its usage line (help text wording is not part of the byte-identical contract).
`python -m pipeline_status diff --help` runs cleanly and describes the positional NAME and the optional --against OTHER.
All pre-existing tests pass unchanged; `python -m unittest discover -s tests` is green in under 5 seconds.
No file other than `pipeline_status/__main__.py` is modified by this task. In particular tests/test_main_subcommands.py is not touched.

tasks.label: A4
tasks.title: [diff/A4] Add README.md section documenting `pipeline-status diff`
tasks.summary: Append/insert one new self-contained section to README.md documenting `pipeline-status diff [--against OTHER] NAME`: description, two-mode contract, four glyph categories, example invocation, exit-code matrix, and the --watch-not-accepted note.
acceptance_criteria: README.md contains a new, self-contained section documenting `pipeline-status diff [--against OTHER] NAME`.

The section covers all six required topics: one-paragraph description; two-mode contract (live vs archive AND archive vs archive); the four glyph categories (+ / - / = / M) each with a one-sentence explanation; one example invocation with example output (one row of each category plus the footer); the exit-code matrix mirroring ADR Decision 8; and a note that --watch and --interval are not accepted with diff.

The new section is placed in alphabetical/feature order relative to existing `archive` and `history` sections, OR appended at the end if those sections do not exist; placement does not modify or reorder any other section.
No existing section, heading, paragraph, or example in README.md is modified, reordered, or deleted by this task.
`git diff master -- README.md` shows only insertions (no deletions); the inserted block is contiguous (one new section, not scattered edits).

Example output in the section matches the format_diff_report shape: rows are `<glyph><space><basename>` with no leading indentation, followed by a single blank line, followed by the footer `Diff: <a> added, <r> removed, <u> unchanged, <m> modified.`.
No file other than README.md is modified. No code change, no new files created, no tests added.
